contaminant.name: Hard Water Mineral Scale
contaminant.description: After evaporation of hard water, mineral deposits form as calcium and magnesium carbonates on surfaces, creating hard, adherent layers that build up over time. These deposits exhibit crystalline patterns and low solubility in water, so they differ from softer rust or greasy contaminants, and on materials like steel or glass they cling tightly while on stones such as marble they merge with natural composition, causing challenges in removal because scraping damages substrates or chemicals corrode metals. Laser cleaning succeeds effectively during pulsed exposure, as energy targets deposits selectively and vaporizes them without affecting underlying durability of aluminum or granite, so treatment preserves surface integrity in observations.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Silicon Dioxide (SiO2) generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
